learningObjectives: Use core house and home vocabulary at A2 level.
Use present and past passive to describe homes and repairs.
Use basic relative clauses with who, which and where.
Understand grammar and vocabulary in a short text about moving home.
Understand conversations about flats and repairs at natural speed.
Write a short message describing your home.
Answer interview questions about where you live.
grammarFocus: Passive voice: present and past simple

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: We bought new furniture for the living room.
We called a worker for a repair in the bathroom.
The rent is due on the first day of the month.
It is a friendly neighbourhood with a park.
We have breakfast on the balcony in summer.
Our car is kept in the garage.
They are paying a mortgage on their house.
The landlord collects the rent every month.
The new tenant moved in last week.
Electricity is the most expensive utility.

grammarReference.structure: Passive voice: present and past simple
grammarReference.explanation: Use passive (be + past participle) when the action is more important than who does it, or when the doer is unknown. Present passive: is/are + past participle. Past passive: was/were + past participle.
examples: The flat is rented by a young family.
Modern appliances are included in the kitchen.
Many houses were built in this neighbourhood in 2010.
commonMistakes: The tap was repair (×) → The tap was repaired (✓)
The rent is pay every month (×) → The rent is paid every month (✓)
The furniture were made (×) → The furniture was made (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use who for people, which for things, and where for places. Defining clauses give essential information (no commas). Non-defining clauses add extra information (with commas).
examples: The tenant who lives upstairs is very friendly.
The flat which we visited has a balcony.
Our neighbourhood, which is near the park, is very quiet.
This is the house where I grew up.
commonMistakes: The man which lives here (×) → The man who lives here (✓)
The flat, we visited (×) → The flat which we visited (✓)
The house who is old (×) → The house which is old (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: House and Home Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve house and home words at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Home Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: Tables, chairs and sofas are types of _____.
questions.explanation: Furniture (đồ nội thất) gồm bàn ghế, sofa.

questions.questionText: A fridge and a washing machine are kitchen _____.
questions.explanation: Appliance (thiết bị) là máy gia dụng.

questions.questionText: When something is broken and fixed, it needs a _____.
questions.explanation: Repair (sửa chữa) khi đồ hỏng được sửa.

questions.questionText: Money you pay to live in a flat each month is the _____.
questions.explanation: Rent (tiền thuê) trả hàng tháng.

questions.questionText: The area around your home is your _____.
questions.explanation: Neighbourhood (khu phố) là vùng quanh nhà.

questions.questionText: We drink coffee on the _____ in the morning sun.
questions.explanation: Balcony (ban công) là nơi ngồi ngoài tầng cao.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the home words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp với nghĩa rõ ràng. Garage và mortgage thường bị nhầm.

pairs.right: a building where you keep a car
money borrowed to buy a house
a person who owns a rented flat
a person who pays rent to live somewhere

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: Electricity and water are examples of a _____.
questions.explanation: Utility (dịch vụ tiện ích) như điện, nước.

questions.questionText: The bedrooms are _____, on the second floor.
questions.explanation: Upstairs (tầng trên) chỉ vị trí phòng ngủ.

questions.questionText: The _____ collects the _____ from the _____.
questions.explanation: Landlord thu rent từ tenant.

questions.questionText: They keep their car in the _____ below the flat.
questions.explanation: Garage (nhà để xe) thường ở tầng dưới.

exercises.title: Apply: Words in Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: The [0] is five hundred a month. | We need a [1] for the tap. | Our [2] is very quiet.
questions.explanation: rent (tiền thuê); repair (sửa chữa); neighbourhood (khu phố).

content.template: The [0] is five hundred a month. We need a [1] for the tap. Our [2] is very quiet.

questions.questionText: Complete: The kitchen [0] are new. | The old [1] was left by the [2].
questions.explanation: appliances (thiết bị); furniture (nội thất); tenant (người thuê).

content.template: The kitchen [0] are new. The old [1] was left by the [2].

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Passive and Relative Clauses
lessons.learningObjective: Use passive voice and basic relative clauses to describe homes.

exercises.title: Learn: Passive and Who/Which
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct word or phrase to complete each sentence.

questions.questionText: The bathroom tap _____ yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + repaired.

questions.questionText: Modern appliances _____ in the kitchen.
questions.explanation: Present passive plural: are included.

questions.questionText: The tenant _____ lives upstairs is very friendly.
questions.explanation: Person → who in relative clause.

questions.questionText: The flat _____ we visited has a large balcony.
questions.explanation: Thing → which in relative clause.

questions.questionText: Many houses in this area _____ in 2010.
questions.explanation: Past passive plural: were built.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: The rent [0] paid on the first day. | The sofa [1] left by the old tenant. | The flats [2] built in 2010.
questions.explanation: is (rent); was (sofa); were (flats) — passive forms.

content.template: The rent [0] paid on the first day. The sofa [1] left by the old tenant. The flats [2] built in 2010.

questions.questionText: Complete: The man [0] is our landlord works nearby. | The house [1] we like has a garage. | This is the street [2] I grew up.
questions.explanation: who (man); which (house); where (street).

content.template: The man [0] is our landlord works nearby. The house [1] we like has a garage. This is the street [2] I grew up.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Our neighbourhood, _____ is near the park, is very safe.
questions.explanation: Non-defining clause for thing/place → which.

questions.questionText: The mortgage _____ every month by my parents.
questions.explanation: Present passive: is paid.

questions.questionText: The repair _____ finished when we arrived home.
questions.explanation: Past passive singular: was finished.

questions.questionText: The landlord _____ rented us the flat is helpful.
questions.explanation: Person → who.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: The tap / was / repaired / yesterday.
questions.explanation: The tap was repaired yesterday. — past passive.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: The flat / which / we visited / has / a balcony.
questions.explanation: The flat which we visited has a balcony. — relative clause with which.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: The appliances / are / made / in / Europe.
questions.explanation: The appliances are made in Europe. — present passive.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Moving Home Article
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short text about moving home and understand grammar and vocabulary in context.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article carefully. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Moving to a New Flat
passage.text:
Last month, Lan and her brother moved into a flat in a quiet neighbourhood. The building is old but the rooms are bright. Their balcony looks over a small park, and the garage downstairs is useful for their father's motorbike.

The landlord explained that the rent includes water, but electricity is extra. The kitchen has modern appliances, including a fridge and a washing machine. However, the sofa in the living room is old furniture that the previous tenant left behind.

On the first weekend, they needed a repair because the bathroom tap was broken. A plumber came quickly and fixed it. Their mother was worried about the mortgage because the family had saved for years to buy this home.

Lan likes the area. She says, "The house which we visited last year was smaller. Our new home, which has a large balcony, is perfect for studying." Her neighbour, who works at the bank, gave useful advice about home insurance. Lan feels happy that the flat was built in 2010 and is well looked after.
passage.imagePrompt: A modern flat interior with balcony view, kitchen appliances, and a quiet residential neighbourhood street with garages.

questions.questionText: What is included in the rent?
questions.explanation: The rent includes water, but electricity is extra.

questions.questionText: Why did they call a plumber?
questions.explanation: Needed a repair because the bathroom tap was broken.

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Article
exercises.instructions: Read the article again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Moving to a New Flat
passage.text:
Last month, Lan and her brother moved into a flat in a quiet neighbourhood. The building is old but the rooms are bright. Their balcony looks over a small park, and the garage downstairs is useful for their father's motorbike.

On the first weekend, they needed a repair because the bathroom tap was broken. A plumber came quickly and fixed it. Their mother was worried about the mortgage because the family had saved for years to buy this home.

Lan likes the area. She says, "The house which we visited last year was smaller. Our new home, which has a large balcony, is perfect for studying." Her neighbour, who works at the bank, gave useful advice about home insurance. Lan feels happy that the flat was built in 2010 and is well looked after.
passage.imagePrompt: A modern flat interior with balcony view, kitchen appliances, and a quiet residential neighbourhood street with garages.

questions.questionText: In the article, 'appliances' refers to _____.
questions.explanation: Fridge and washing machine — thiết bị gia dụng.

choices.text: machines like a fridge and washing machine

questions.questionText: Why was Lan's mother worried?
questions.explanation: Worried about the mortgage — vay mua nhà.

choices.text: Because the rent was too high

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the article one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

passage.title: Moving to a New Flat
passage.text:

passage.imagePrompt: A modern flat interior with balcony view, kitchen appliances, and a quiet residential neighbourhood street with garages.

questions.questionText: What is the article mainly about?
questions.explanation: Bài kể Lan chuyển đến căn hộ mới và trải nghiệm đầu tiên.

choices.text: How to get a mortgage from a bank
Lan's family moving into a new flat

questions.questionText: Put the events in order: (1) Repair in bathroom (2) Landlord explains rent (3) Family moves in
questions.explanation: Chuyển vào → landlord giải thích → sửa tap cuối tuần.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Article
exercises.instructions: Use what you read. Match each item to the correct fact.

questions.questionText: Match each item from the article to the correct fact.
questions.explanation: Mỗi mục khớp với chi tiết trong bài.

pairs.right: Old sofa left by previous tenant

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Home Conversations
lessons.learningObjective: Understand details in conversations about flats and repairs at natural speed.

exercises.title: Learn: Flat Viewing Call
exercises.instructions: Listen to the phone call. Answer the questions.

script.title: Flat Viewing Phone Call
script.setting: Phone call between tenant and landlord

lines.speaker: Lan
lines.text: Hello. I'm calling about the flat on Oak Street. Is it still available?

lines.speaker: Landlord
lines.text: Yes. It has two bedrooms, a balcony and a garage. The rent is five hundred pounds a month.

lines.speaker: Lan
lines.text: Are the appliances included? We need a washing machine.

lines.speaker: Landlord
lines.text: Yes, the washing machine and fridge are included. The neighbourhood is very quiet.

lines.speaker: Lan
lines.text: Great. Can we visit on Saturday? My email is lan.nguyen@email.com.

script.audioNotes: Clear phone call, numbers and email spelled slowly. Natural pace. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: What is Lan calling about?
questions.explanation: Calling about the flat on Oak Street.

questions.questionText: How much is the rent per month?
questions.explanation: The rent is five hundred pounds a month.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Viewing Call
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same phone call. Answer the next questions.

script.title: Flat Viewing Phone Call
script.setting: Phone call between tenant and landlord

lines.speaker: Lan
lines.text: Hello. I'm calling about the flat on Oak Street. Is it still available?

lines.speaker: Landlord
lines.text: Yes. It has two bedrooms, a balcony and a garage. The rent is five hundred pounds a month.

lines.speaker: Lan
lines.text: Are the appliances included? We need a washing machine.

lines.speaker: Landlord
lines.text: Yes, the washing machine and fridge are included. The neighbourhood is very quiet.

lines.speaker: Lan
lines.text: Great. Can we visit on Saturday? My email is lan.nguyen@email.com.

script.audioNotes: Clear phone call, numbers and email spelled slowly. Natural pace. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: Which appliances are included?
questions.explanation: Washing machine and fridge are included.

questions.questionText: When does Lan want to visit the flat?
questions.explanation: Can we visit on Saturday?

exercises.title: Check: Repair Visit
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new conversation. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Repair Visit Conversation

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Thank you for coming. The tap in the bathroom was broken yesterday.

lines.speaker: Plumber
lines.text: No problem. It will be repaired in about thirty minutes.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: The furniture in here is old, but the appliances in the kitchen are new.

lines.speaker: Plumber
lines.text: I see. Many flats in this building were built in 2010. The garage is downstairs.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Perfect. Should I pay now or later?

script.audioNotes: Indoor conversation, moderate natural speed with brief pauses. Approx. 38 seconds.

questions.questionText: What needs to be repaired?
questions.explanation: The tap in the bathroom was broken.

questions.questionText: How long will the repair take?
questions.explanation: Repaired in about thirty minutes.

questions.questionText: When were many flats in the building built?
questions.explanation: Many flats were built in 2010.

exercises.title: Apply: Match What You Heard
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the flat viewing call. Match each item to the correct detail.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Message About Your Home
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short message describing your home using unit vocabulary and passive forms.

exercises.title: Learn: Home Description Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: balcony, rent, furniture, appliance, neighbourhood.

questions.questionText: Complete: Hi Sam, | Our [0] is quiet and friendly. | The [1] is four hundred a month. | We have a small [2].
questions.explanation: neighbourhood (khu phố); rent (tiền thuê); balcony (ban công).

content.template:
Hi Sam,

Our [0] is quiet and friendly. The [1] is four hundred a month. We have a small [2].

exercises.title: Practice: Build Home Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: The kitchen / was / painted / last year.
questions.explanation: The kitchen was painted last year. — past passive.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: The flat / which / we rent / has / a garage.
questions.explanation: The flat which we rent has a garage. — relative clause.

exercises.title: Check: Message About Your Home
exercises.instructions: Write a message to a friend. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: Write a short message to your friend Sam describing where you live now.
prompts: Say what type of home you live in (flat or house).
Describe one or two features (balcony, garage, appliances, furniture).
End the message in a friendly way.
successCriteria: Uses passive or relative clause if possible
Uses at least two unit vocabulary words

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Sam,

I live in a flat which has a small balcony and a garage downstairs. The kitchen appliances are new, but the furniture is quite old. Our neighbourhood is quiet and friendly. The rent is paid on the first day every month.

See you soon,
Lan

grammar.criteria: Uses passive or relative clauses (who/which/where) correctly if attempted.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses home words (furniture, appliance, rent, balcony, neighbourhood) appropriately.

organization.criteria: Message has greeting, description and a friendly closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Describes home and neighbourhood clearly; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Dear Ana, | The tap [0] repaired yesterday. | The man [1] is our landlord is kind. | Best, | Minh
questions.explanation: was (past passive); who (relative clause for person).

content.template:
Dear Ana,

The tap [0] repaired yesterday. The man [1] is our landlord is kind.

Best,
Minh

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Your Home
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about your home using unit vocabulary and grammar.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Do you live in a house or a flat? You say:
questions.explanation: Trả lời trực tiếp loại nhà ở.

choices.text: I live in a rent with my family.
choices.distractorNote: Rent is payment not home type

choices.text: I live in a flat with my family.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What is your favourite room? You say:
questions.explanation: Mô tả phòng yêu thích với balcony hoặc view.

choices.text: My favourite room is the garage because it has a balcony.
My favourite room is the living room because it has a balcony.
My favourite repair is the living room.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Is your home old or new? You say:
questions.explanation: Passive: was built — mô tả năm xây.

choices.text: It is quite new. It built in 2015.
It is quite new. It was built in 2015.
It is quite new. It was build in 2015.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best phrase you would say in each situation.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Tell me about your neighbourhood. You say:
questions.explanation: Neighbourhood + đặc điểm — cấu trúc phỏng vấn A2.

choices.text: My mortgage is quiet and there is a park nearby.
My neighbourhood is quiet and there is a park nearby.
My appliance is quiet and there is a park nearby.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Who do you pay rent to? You say:
questions.explanation: Landlord là chủ nhà nhận rent.

choices.text: We pay rent to our tenant every month.
We pay rent to our landlord every month.
We pay rent to our garage every month.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Describe a person who lives near you. You say:
questions.explanation: Relative clause with who for neighbour.

choices.text: I have a neighbour which works at the local shop.
I have a neighbour who works at the local shop.
I have a neighbour where works at the local shop.

exercises.title: Check: Home Interview
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions about where you live. Speak for up to two minutes.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each home situation.

questions.questionText: Your landlord asks if the appliances work. You say:
questions.explanation: Mô tả appliances hoạt động tốt.

choices.text: Yes, the mortgage and rent work well.
Yes, the fridge and washing machine work well.
Yes, the neighbourhood and garage work well.

questions.questionText: A friend asks where you park your car. You say:
questions.explanation: Garage downstairs — trả lời tự nhiên.

choices.text: We park it on the balcony.
We park it in the garage downstairs.
We park it in the mortgage.

questions.questionText: You are describing the flat you want to rent. You say:
questions.explanation: Relative clause: flat which has...

choices.text: I want a flat who has a balcony and a quiet neighbourhood.
I want a flat which has a balcony and a quiet neighbourhood.
I want a flat where has a balcony and a quiet neighbourhood.
